author: jsign
content: As think as <@628018527470616606> and <@126385519469789184> are refering to -- prob we will need guest programs to be architected in a way to do what SP1 originally named "subblock proving".

author: jsign
content: This does not necessarily mean requiring changes in the protocol.

author: jsign
content: You can think more like splitting the execution and proving into "BAL parts"

author: jsign
content: Or also as having intermediate roots which was the original SP1 idea.

author: jsign
content: So at least you paralellize the RISCV emulation part which is currently mainly single threaded.

author: jsign
content: <@402080271563751435> is planning to explore this more in detail soon to understand at which gas limits this might be required -- but definitely it will come to a point wher simply single-threaded RISCV emulation at a particular gas limit will be a bottleneck

author: joomanhan_41909
content: Thanks it all make sense. is this already included on zkEVM or planned?

author: jsign
content: It is kind of the same parallelization that ELs do using BALs, but doing proof aggregation instread of using "threads" say.

author: jsign
content: I think for now is more in the experimental phase starting soon, since we need to udnerstand better at which gas limit we will definitely need it.

author: jsign
content: The answer might change dpeending on the zkEVM too -- some zkEVM might have a slower or faster emulation speed, so the answer can change.

author: jsign
content: But I think starting with one guest program and one zkEVM is a good start to start having an idea about this topic.

author: omerfirmak
content: I can give it a try when I have the time.

author: joomanhan_41909
content: <@774033563732541451> Thanks for explain! all questions are cleared on my end 🙂

author: joomanhan_41909
content: do we have an articles/writings i can dig a bit more about this?

author: omerfirmak
content:
> Or also as having intermediate roots which was the original SP1 idea.
I thought this was needed but forgot to consider BALs. It should be fairly straightforward if BALs are utilized. Only BAL's read-only entries might be a little problematic.
